philosophy: Leopold/Visser-style conviction engine: concentrated, anti-momentum, rotates toward what hasn't moved yet, repositions decisively on score changes. Long-only adaptation of a hedge-fund book: structural guardrails (floor, cash, capped top) substitute for the put-hedge a real fund would use.

two_spines.ai_bottleneck: Physical AI inputs: power generation, grid/equipment, leading-edge compute, memory/interconnect, data-center real estate.
two_spines.monetary_scarcity: Hard money in a debasement regime: gold, silver, Bitcoin. Co-equal with the AI spine in S1 scoring. NOTE: scope is tightly limited to these two categories — a random growth stock does NOT get to claim 'scarcity'.

asset_type_routing.note: S4 (catalyst) and S5 (entry) are sourced differently by asset type.

company.S4_source: Company catalyst calendar: earnings dates, >100MW power contracts, product launches, policy events.
company.S5_source: Price/momentum: % off 52-wk high, RSI. ANTI-MOMENTUM: parabolic names penalized hard.

macro_hardmoney.S4_source: Each asset scored on its OWN driver, NOT a generic inflation tag: SLV=industrial deficit + gold/silver ratio + tariff/demand; GLDM=central-bank buying + dollar/real-yields; IBIT=ETF flow direction + supply absorption. Liquid prediction markets (Kalshi/Polymarket on CPI/PPI/Fed) inform the regime backdrop.
macro_hardmoney.S5_source: Cycle position + how far it has run. Un-run / off-highs = high. Parabolic = penalized.

asset_type_routing.macro_note: COPX is classified as COMPANY/power-industrial (grid+EV+humanoid copper), NOT monetary-scarcity. It does NOT get the inflation catalyst.

anti_momentum_rule.principle: A name being up large is a REASON TO OWN LESS, not more — the asymmetry has decayed. This is the core Visser/Leopold rotation discipline: move toward what hasn't moved yet.
anti_momentum_rule.S5_guidance: Penalize VELOCITY + STAGE EXHAUSTION, not proximity-to-high. A structural winner is near its highs the entire way up (MU/BE were near highs through a 5-14x run) - proximity alone is NOT a penalty. Bands: (1) Down/pullback-in-uptrend, thesis intact = 85-100. (2) Off-highs, hasn't run recently = 70-84. (3) Near highs in a STEADY structural uptrend, theme still early/binding (high S2) = 65-80 - this is the catch-the-trend-early zone, do NOT penalize. (4) Near highs but theme STAGE PEAKED (low S2) OR retail ETF flooding in = 45-60. (5) Vertical BLOWOFF (up multiples in <1yr, RSI>75, parabolic last weeks) = 25-45 regardless of stage. KEY: S2 (stage) carries the 'is this run sustainable' judgment; S5 should only hammer blowoff velocity + late-stage highs, never the steady early-stage new-high grind.
anti_momentum_rule.no_fundamental_cushion: Signals stay INDEPENDENT. A strong thesis (high S1) does NOT soften the entry penalty. S1 already gets 25% direct weight; letting it also cushion S5 would double-count. A best-in-class name that has run hard lands at a poor entry score honestly.
anti_momentum_rule._refined: 5/27/26 - separated 'near highs in early-stage uptrend' (no penalty) from 'blowoff velocity / late-stage highs' (penalty). Prevents double-penalizing structural winners that S2 already vets.

capex_multiplier.note: Portfolio-wide circuit breaker on the AI/company spine.

convergence_voices._added: 2026-07-02 (tiered 3-voice convergence). Widens convergence from Visser-only cross-source to CROSS-VOICE. Aschenbrenner dropped from the auto-count.
convergence_voices._doc: lenses_pointing (0-3) is computed UPSTREAM in pull_candidates.cjs from BASE_PORTFOLIO membership + the voice_mentions ledger, then pasted into the rescore SignalInput rows. Payout is unchanged: convergence_bonus_values still maps 2->60, 3->100 (the 0.6/1.0 curve). Only positive legs count (conviction in count_convictions); 'Mentioned'/'Caution' do not. ZaStocks is corroboration-only: his leg counts ONLY if Visser or Camillo also point at the name, he never seats a name alone, and he never trips the voice_floor by himself.

weighting.method: Conviction-proportional: weight proportional to (composite - floor_score)^k. k solved each run so the TOP name hits target_top_pct.
weighting.note: min_position_pct keeps every included name (composite>=45) at a real >=2% so a strong regime can't zero out a whole theme.

aggressive.note: The live AlphaPlaybook book. Concentrated, conviction-weighted, no single-stock cap.

conservative.note: Offline mirror (19-name form). Gentler convexity = more diversified. Same code, lower target_top.

conservative_compact.note: Sub-10 redesign (6/9/26): metals / BTC-proxy / oil / pharma / AI / cash. target_top 16 because the 11% top was calibrated for 19 holdings and degenerates to flat weights (k~0.2, conviction-order inversion) at 9 names. OFFLINE BY DESIGN — standing decision, not an API-capacity constraint.

risk_caps.single_stock_cap: SUPERSEDED 2026-06-01 by coverage_discount (Rule B). Cap no longer applied; redundancy haircut on weighting_score does the job via scoring.
risk_caps.note: Concentration risk is managed by (a) target_top_pct=18 not 20+, (b) the 2% floor for breadth, (c) the cash floor. These substitute for the put-hedge a real fund uses.

exit_rules.thesis_break: Exit regardless of price if the bottleneck is resolved/bypassed.

gates._doc: Phase 2 asymmetric-S5 gates. Exit side (2a): TRIM rides the composite (no extra cut), EXIT forces out, accelerant adds +1 entry-band cut for confirmed company breakdowns; Rule C upgrades an accelerated trim to a full EXIT when the theme is ETF-covered. Entry pause (2b) is applied in the normalizer.

rule_c._added: 2026-06-09 (approved). Reference case: CEG (theme held via AIPO).
rule_c._doc: Accelerant + ETF theme coverage = EXIT, not floor. When the exit accelerant fires (company, not exit_exempt, in the trim zone, below-200 + mom-down) AND the name's theme is already held via a thematic ETF, the name exits instead of parking at a floor weight — a broken single name whose theme you own diversified is clutter, not a position. Coverage is read from coverage_discount.redundant_by_sleeve (single source of truth shared with Rule B) OR the etf_redundant input flag. voice_floor_exempt: a voice_conviction=True name never Rule-C exits (the voice floor backstop wins). Narrow by construction: requires every accelerant condition PLUS coverage.

conviction_proximity_carveout._added: 2026-06-15 (Spec A). Motivated by SLV sitting on its 200-DMA (+0.1%) with Visser reaffirming/buying.
conviction_proximity_carveout._doc: A HELD macro_hardmoney name below its 200-DMA is EXEMPT from the entry pause + S5 floor if it is within band_pct of the 200-DMA AND voice_conviction=True. At-trend + voice-buying != downtrend. Re-arms automatically if price falls outside the band (the pause/floor returns). Removes the FREEZE only; the name flows to its normal engine weight, no boost (fix scores not weights). Narrow by design: hard money only, tight band, voice-gated. Does NOT touch companies, deep-downtrend hard money (IBIT -27%, ETHA -43% are outside the band), or non-voice names.

stage_decay._doc: Weight bleeds as a stage cools. Multiplier on WEIGHTING SCORE only (composite/tier/exit triggers unchanged), keyed to S2 stage timing. ws = floor_score + (composite-floor_score)*mult. Stacks MULTIPLICATIVELY with Rule B lambda (a covered, cooling single name gets both). EXEMPT: macro_hardmoney (not a 5-stage AI name) and ETFs (already diversified) -> mult 1.0. Effect on the 6/9 book is modest (GLW 9.5->8.5, BE/HOOD -0.5) because most names are hard money, ETFs, or in binding stages; value is FORWARD-acting (auto-trim as a stage rolls binding->working->cooling). NOTE: this is the bolt-on-multiplier implementation; the considered alternative was raising S2's composite weight (cleaner re: double-count) -- chose the multiplier for surgical control + hard-money/ETF exemption, which a composite-weight change can't target. Watch for interaction with cash floor: protect cash in the normalizer call.

voice_floor._doc: A name with reaffirmed tracked-voice conviction (SignalInput.voice_conviction=True) holds a minimum weight (floor_pct) and is exempt from the exit gate's force-out. The voice's conviction still enters PRIMARILY via the convergence score; this floor is the backstop so cooling/parabolic brakes can't zero a live-voice name. Bounded + evidence-gated (flag set only on recent reaffirmation). APPROVED LIVE 6/9/26.

s5_ladder._doc: Phase 2.5: S5 position from 20/50/200 DMA ladder (auto), velocity/blowoff penalty from RSI+1yr (gathered). DMAs set the band; RSI+velocity set the penalty. NEVER penalize DMA distance (5/27 proximity-penalty trap).

velocity_penalty._doc: Velocity = stretch ABOVE 50-DMA (price vs dma50), NOT trailing-1yr level. Penalty x0.5 in working/binding themes (s2>=working_theme_s2_thr), x1.0 in peaked. RSI>=70 guardrail is separate. Replaces vel_parabolic/extended_1y (6/22/26).

turnover_note: Engine repositions decisively on score changes (Leopold-like). In TAXABLE accounts, rebalance only when target moves >3-4% to limit short-term gains. The engine outputs targets; the human decides when to actually trade.

macro_regime_tilt._added: 2026-06-20 (Macro Regime Gate Part 2). Option B +/-10% tilt.
macro_regime_tilt._doc: Tilt on the WEIGHTING SCORE only (eff_mult), applied ONLY when the CPI macro regime is above 4% (regime.above from the snapshot 2-of-3 vote, passed into score_ticker). Boosts real-asset/hard-money names, penalizes rate-sensitive duration, neutral elsewhere. Composite/tier/exit unchanged (like Rule B + stage-decay). SGOV/cash untouched. Below 4% = neutral everywhere; no inverse tilt. Stacks multiplicatively with Rule B lambda and stage-decay. UNLIKE stage-decay/Rule B this DOES apply to ETFs (IBIT/GLDM/SLV boost, XSD penalize) -- taxonomy is by macro-regime behavior, not asset type.

coverage_discount._added: 2026-06-01 (Portfolio Construction session) — 'single-name redundancy discount' / Rule B.
coverage_discount.rule: A single-stock holding that is THEME-COVERED by a held thematic ETF (its theme is ownable diversified inside a fund you already hold) gets an idiosyncratic-risk haircut applied to its WEIGHTING SCORE only: weighting_score = floor_score + (composite - floor_score) * lambda. composite itself is UNCHANGED, so entry-tier labels and exit/trim triggers are unaffected.
coverage_discount.exempt: ETFs (lambda=1.0) and single names whose THEME has no held ETF (e.g. optical: GLW/MRVL — appearing at trivial weight inside a broad basket like XSD does NOT count as theme coverage).
coverage_discount.notes: Lands CEG ~7.5% (agg) / ~6.5% (con) — replaces the ad-hoc 12% single-stock cap. lambda is the judgment knob: 0.80→~7%, 0.814→~7.5%, 0.83→~8%. 6/9/26: CEG exited via Rule C; this list now also feeds gates.rule_c coverage (shared source of truth). OPEN FLAG: BE's etf_redundant input flag was not set in the 6/9 rescore runs — fold back in at next rescore or rule that the voice floor supersedes it for live-voice names.
